seo.pageTitle: East Jeju Sunrise Peak, Folk Village & Hamdeok Bus Day Tour | AtoC Korea
seo.metaDescription: A classic East Jeju bus day tour covering Ilchul Land, Seongeup Folk Village, Seongsan Ilchulbong, the Haenyeo Show, and Hamdeok Beach. A strong first-time route for travelers who want Jeju’s iconic east-side nature and culture in one day.

catalog_card.title: East Jeju Sunrise Peak, Folk Village & Hamdeok Bus Day Tour
catalog_card.subtitle: Lava cave atmosphere, living village culture, UNESCO scenery, haenyeo heritage, and a signature beach in one classic east-side route, now with one hour of free lunch time near Seongsan.
catalog_card.shortCardDescription: A full-day East Jeju bus route that links cave scenery, traditional village culture, Seongsan Ilchulbong, living haenyeo heritage, and Hamdeok Beach, with one hour of free lunch time added near Seongsan. It is built for travelers who want strong iconic coverage with a lower price barrier.

headlineLine1: East Jeju Sunrise Peak, Folk Village

hero.tagline: From lava-cave atmosphere and a living folk village to UNESCO coastal scenery, Jeju haenyeo culture, and Hamdeok’s clear water, this classic East Jeju bus route delivers iconic variety at a practical price point, with one hour of free lunch time included near Seongsan.

galleryItems.atmosphere: The day's signature UNESCO landmark and strongest iconic view
galleryItems.alt: Seongsan Ilchulbong rising above the East Jeju coast

galleryItems.atmosphere: A cooler lava-cave opening with indoor value and unusual texture
galleryItems.alt: Micheongul Cave inside Ilchul Land in East Jeju

galleryItems.atmosphere: Living heritage and old Jeju village character
galleryItems.alt: Traditional houses at Seongeup Folk Village in Jeju

galleryItems.atmosphere: A bright, relaxed finish with Jeju's signature clear water feeling
galleryItems.alt: Bright coastal atmosphere inspired by Hamdeok Beach in Jeju

itineraryStops.name: Ilchul Land
itineraryStops.description: Ilchul Land works well as an opening stop because it combines easy garden circulation with the cooler Micheongul lava-cave section. It gives the day an early indoor-outdoor balance before the route moves deeper into East Jeju's cultural and coastal landmarks.
highlights: A flexible first stop with both outdoor paths and cave atmosphere
Useful early rain-buffer compared with a fully exposed route opening
Good variety for mixed-age groups
A different visual tone from the coast-heavy second half
timeUsed: Garden walk and Micheongul cave viewing
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Starting here gives the route a softer operational opening and improves weather resilience before the village, UNESCO climb, and exposed coastline sections.

visitBasics.hours: 08:30–19:00 / Micheongul cave admission generally until 1 hour before sunset
visitBasics.admission: Commonly ticketed attraction; keep live pricing aligned with current checkout setup

smartNotes.photo: The cave textures and garden framing work better than rushed single-point photos, so this stop benefits from steady pacing.
smartNotes.facilities: Facilities are stronger here than at most pure viewpoint stops.
smartNotes.tip: This is one of the route's best buffers when wind or light rain makes later outdoor stops less comfortable.

itineraryStops.name: Seongeup Folk Village
itineraryStops.description: Seongeup adds cultural depth that many scenic-only routes miss. Because it is a living village rather than a staged set, the stop gives travelers a clearer sense of old Jeju homes, stone walls, and daily life traditions.
highlights: One of East Jeju's strongest culture-focused stops
A living village with real local heritage value
Adds storytelling depth between nature landmarks
Works well for first-time visitors who want more than scenery alone
timeUsed: Village orientation and short guided walk
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: It breaks up the nature-heavy flow and adds heritage context before the route turns toward Seongsan and the coast.

visitBasics.hours: Open village environment; access patterns can vary by site area and local operation
visitBasics.admission: Generally free to enter the village area, though some shops or experiences may be optional

convenience.restroom: Available in or near visitor areas

smartNotes.photo: Stone walls, thatched roofs, and side-lane composition usually read better than wide rushed overview shots.
smartNotes.facilities: This is a cultural walk stop rather than a large modern complex.
smartNotes.tip: Keep the stop focused on village character and guide context rather than overextending shopping time.

itineraryStops.name: Seongsan Ilchulbong
itineraryStops.description: Seongsan Ilchulbong is the route's most iconic landmark and one of Jeju's strongest first-time stops. The summit trail is short in distance but clearly steep, so the stop feels more active than most bus-tour landmarks.
highlights: UNESCO World Natural Heritage site and one of Jeju's signature views
High payoff for first-time visitors
Short but steep summit climb with strong visual reward
A landmark that anchors the whole route
timeUsed: Ascent or lower-area sightseeing depending on pace and condition
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: This is the natural centerpiece of an East Jeju classic route, so it works best near midday when visibility is often still stable and the day's momentum is high.

visitBasics.hours: Seasonal operation; for example, Nov–Feb 00:00–18:00, Mar–Apr & Sep–Oct 05:00–19:00, May–Aug 04:30–20:00, with ticketing ending earlier
visitBasics.closed: First Monday of each month, or the following day if Monday is a public holiday
visitBasics.admission: Ticketed summit section; keep live pricing aligned with current checkout setup
visitBasics.walking: Moderate to difficult if climbing to the top

smartNotes.photo: Wide crater-and-sea framing is usually the strongest visual payoff.
smartNotes.facilities: This is a major landmark stop with solid basic visitor support.
smartNotes.tip: Not every traveler needs to do the full summit climb; the lower-area coastal section can still work for guests avoiding heavier stairs.

itineraryStops.name: Lunch Break (Free Choice)
itineraryStops.description: Enjoy one hour of free lunch time near Seongsan. This flexible meal break lets travelers choose a restaurant that fits their taste and pace before continuing to the haenyeo segment.
highlights: One full hour for lunch
Free-choice meal stop
Placed after Seongsan climbing time
Better pacing before the afternoon program
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: This break sits naturally after the Seongsan stop, where many travelers want time to recover from the climb and eat before the afternoon coastal segment.

smartNotes.facilities: Restaurant options vary by day and traffic flow
smartNotes.tip: A simple local meal works best if you want a relaxed pace.

itineraryStops.name: Haenyeo Show
itineraryStops.description: The haenyeo stop adds something few Jeju routes can match: living culture happening on the coast rather than inside a display panel. Because the performance is weather-sensitive, the tour should treat it as a priority highlight with a clear fallback plan.
highlights: One of the best ways to connect Jeju sightseeing with living local culture
Stronger storytelling value than a standard photo stop
Directly tied to Jeju's haenyeo heritage
Memorable when sea conditions allow normal operation
timeUsed: Move to the viewing area and guide context
Observe the performance if operating normally
If cancelled for weather, replace with Jeju Haenyeo Museum
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Positioning this after Seongsan keeps the coastal storytelling connected and makes the cultural flow feel natural rather than inserted.

visitBasics.hours: Planned around the 14:00 show window on this product; actual operation is subject to season and weather conditions
visitBasics.closed: May be cancelled or adjusted in rough sea or poor weather
visitBasics.admission: Usually viewed without a major ticket barrier, but keep live setup aligned with operations

convenience.restroom: Basic facilities vary by viewing point and nearby shops
convenience.parking: Operational drop-off and parking depend on day-of traffic

smartNotes.photo: Use wider framing and avoid expecting a perfectly fixed stage environment.
smartNotes.facilities: This is a live coastal viewing stop, so comfort is more weather-dependent than at museums or indoor sites.
smartNotes.tip: If the show is cancelled, the Jeju Haenyeo Museum is the cleanest substitution because it preserves the route's cultural logic.

itineraryStops.name: Hamdeok Beach
itineraryStops.description: Hamdeok gives the day a bright, easy finish. After the more structured stops at Seongeup, Seongsan, and the haenyeo segment, the white sand and clear water create a simpler closing mood that many travelers find refreshing.
highlights: One of Jeju's best-known beaches
Clear water and easy seafront walking
A relaxed visual finish after a fuller sightseeing day
Good family and couple appeal
timeUsed: Regroup for return to Jeju City
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: It works naturally as a late-day decompression stop before the return drive to Jeju City.

smartNotes.photo: The water color reads best in wider beach shots and side angles rather than tight front-on framing.
smartNotes.facilities: Amenities and surrounding cafés are part of why this stop feels easy and finish-friendly.
smartNotes.tip: In strong wind or rough weather, reduce expectations for a long beach stay and keep the stop focused.

routeShapeIntro.title: Why this East Jeju route feels balanced
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: It opens with flexible cave-and-garden pacing, adds real heritage context, peaks with Seongsan, pauses for one hour of free lunch time, carries that story into the haenyeo segment, and finishes with a light beach stop before the return to Jeju City.

bestFor: Travelers who want iconic East Jeju coverage
Visitors who want both natural landmarks and cultural context in one day
lessIdeal: Travelers avoiding stairs or uphill walking
Travelers who want a very slow luxury pace
Anyone seeking a mostly indoor or fully weather-proof route

routeLogicSections.title: Why this bus route works well

items.label: High iconic coverage for the price
items.detail: It connects one of Jeju's strongest UNESCO landmarks with heritage, living culture, and a signature beach without the price jump of a private format.

items.label: The route does not feel one-note
items.detail: Cave atmosphere, village heritage, summit scenery, haenyeo culture, and beach time each give the day a clearly different mood.

items.label: The finish gets easier, not harder
items.detail: After the active Seongsan segment, the haenyeo stop and Hamdeok finish help the day land more comfortably.

routeLogicSections.title: How the walking load actually feels

items.label: Mostly manageable, with one clearly demanding stop
items.detail: Ilchul Land, Seongeup, the haenyeo stop, and Hamdeok are manageable for most travelers, while Seongsan Ilchulbong is the one stop that adds real stairs and incline.

items.label: More active than a pure panorama bus route
items.detail: The route stays within normal day-tour range, but it is not a purely passive coach sightseeing day because of Seongsan.

items.label: Mixed-age groups can usually handle it
items.detail: The route works for many mixed-age groups as long as they are comfortable either climbing Seongsan or using the lower-area option.

items.label: The route is more resilient than a fully exposed east-coast plan
items.detail: Ilchul Land gives useful indoor-outdoor flexibility at the start, and the Jeju Haenyeo Museum is a clean cultural fallback if the live show is cancelled.

items.label: Seongsan and Hamdeok remain weather-sensitive
items.detail: Wind, rain, and visibility changes most strongly affect the UNESCO climb and beach finish.

items.label: Operational substitutions should preserve the story
items.detail: If weather forces a change, the best adjustment is not random replacement but keeping the East Jeju culture-and-coast logic intact.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Pickup and Return Times
practicalAccordionItems.preview: 4 fixed pickup points + 5 city drop-off points
content: Pickup: 08:30 Ocean Suites Hotel
Pickup: 08:45 Jeju Airport, 3rd Floor Gate 3
Pickup: 08:55 Lotte City Hotel Jeju
Pickup: 09:05 Shilla Duty Free Shop
Drop-off: Ocean Suites Hotel
Drop-off: Jeju Airport, 3rd Floor Gate 3
Drop-off: Lotte City Hotel Jeju
Drop-off: Shilla Duty Free Shop
Extra drop-off available at Dongmun Market
Expected return to Jeju City around 17:40–18:20 depending on traffic, weather, and show operation
Please arrive at least 10 minutes early at your selected pickup point

practicalAccordionItems.title: Walking and Terrain
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Moderate overall, with Seongsan as the key active stop
content: Ilchul Land, Seongeup Folk Village, the haenyeo stop, and Hamdeok are generally manageable for most travelers
Seongsan Ilchulbong is the most physically demanding stop because of stairs and uphill sections
If needed, some travelers can limit themselves to the lower area at Seongsan instead of pushing to the summit
Comfortable walking shoes with grip are strongly recommended

practicalAccordionItems.title: Weather and Route Adjustments
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Most affected by wind at Seongsan, the haenyeo stop, and Hamdeok
content: Light rain: the tour usually still operates
Heavy rain, rough sea, or strong wind: the haenyeo show may be cancelled and replaced with Jeju Haenyeo Museum
Seongsan Ilchulbong summit access and comfort level can be affected by weather and crowd conditions
If a stop becomes unsafe or impractical, timing or stop order may be adjusted while preserving the East Jeju route logic

practicalAccordionItems.title: What to Wear and Bring
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Walking shoes, layers, and wind protection are the basics
content: Comfortable walking shoes with good grip
A light jacket or windproof outer layer
Sun protection and a hat on clear days
Water bottle
A small umbrella or light rain layer if the forecast is mixed

practicalAccordionItems.title: What’s Included / Not Included
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Guide, vehicle, fixed pickup, and standard sightseeing support
content: Professional English-speaking guide
Shared bus transportation
Fixed pickup and drop-off in Jeju City
Basic guided sightseeing support
Admission fees are not included unless otherwise specified at checkout
Lunch is not included unless otherwise specified
Personal expenses, café purchases, and optional shopping are not included
Guide gratuities are optional

bookingTrustItems.title: Fixed Jeju City pickups
bookingTrustItems.description: Four meeting points with scheduled times

bookingTrustItems.title: Structured bus day
bookingTrustItems.description: Clear stop order and return window

bookingTrustItems.title: Full East Jeju coverage
bookingTrustItems.description: Iconic sights at a bus-tour price point

bookingSupportSteps.title: Leave contact details you actually use
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Add a phone number, WhatsApp number, and LINE ID or add-friend link you check every day. If we cannot reach you, the guide may not be able to confirm the meet-up in time.

bookingSupportSteps.title: Confirmation email first
bookingSupportSteps.detail: You get a booking confirmation by email: route summary, price, and how your pickup or meeting is arranged.

bookingSupportSteps.title: Your guide contacts you
bookingSupportSteps.detail: The day before departure, your guide reaches you directly on WhatsApp, LINE, or phone to confirm time, the exact meeting point, and any last details.

bookingSupportSteps.title: At the meeting point
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Arrive at least 10 minutes before your time; the bus departs on schedule. Follow the time your guide shared with you.

bookingSupportSteps.title: Return & support
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Return to the fixed drop-off points in Jeju City. Message us if you need help after the day.

seasonalVariations.name: Spring
seasonalVariations.description: Seongeup and Hamdeok usually feel lighter and more colorful. Visibility can make Seongsan particularly rewarding on clear days.

seasonalVariations.name: Summer
seasonalVariations.description: Hamdeok becomes a stronger crowd-appeal stop. Heat and humidity make Seongsan feel more physically demanding, while Ilchul Land's shaded caves gain value.

seasonalVariations.name: Autumn
seasonalVariations.description: Comfortable walking temperatures generally improve overall route feel. Clearer air can lift Seongsan and Hamdeok photo quality.

seasonalVariations.name: Winter
seasonalVariations.description: Wind exposure matters more at Seongsan, the haenyeo stop, and Hamdeok. The cultural stops become relatively more important for route comfort.

faqItems.q: Is this a good East Jeju tour for first-time visitors?
faqItems.a: Yes. It covers several of East Jeju's best-known highlights in one day and keeps the price lower than a private tour, which makes it a strong first-visit bus option.

faqItems.q: How difficult is the walking on this route?
faqItems.a: Most stops are manageable for average travelers. The one stop that clearly feels more active is Seongsan Ilchulbong, which includes stairs and uphill walking if you go toward the summit.

faqItems.q: What happens if the haenyeo show is cancelled?
faqItems.a: If sea or weather conditions prevent normal operation, the route should switch to Jeju Haenyeo Museum so the cultural logic of the day is preserved.
This JSON is structured for a bus-tour setup where admission is not included unless your checkout configuration says otherwise. Keep the live product setup aligned with your actual offer.

featured.name: Emma
featured.comment: A very satisfying East Jeju route for first-time visitors. Seongsan was the big landmark, but the haenyeo story and Seongeup made the day feel richer than a scenery-only tour.

featured.name: Michael
featured.comment: Good value for a bus tour. The Seongsan climb was the hardest part, but the rest of the stops were well spaced and Hamdeok was a nice way to finish.

walking_notes: Overall walking is moderate for a bus tour rather than a hiking tour.
Ilchul Land, Seongeup, the haenyeo stop, and Hamdeok are manageable for most travelers.
Seongsan Ilchulbong is the steepest and most physically demanding stop because of stairs and uphill sections.
Comfortable grip shoes are strongly recommended, especially in wet or windy weather.

first time east jeju day trip

matching_metadata.note: Scores were tuned for a classic East Jeju bus-tour format: very high iconic and storytelling fit, moderate walking overall, and moderate rain resilience because Ilchul Land adds indoor-outdoor balance and the haenyeo stop has a museum fallback, while Seongsan and Hamdeok remain weather-sensitive.
